artifacts.content:
Dad,

Just wanted to give you a quick update while I’m sitting here at the kitchen table waiting on Claire to finish her robotics homework.

Suite B at 1542 W 32nd is still basically frozen in time from when Sean shut down FastTrack Tax & Notary back in April 2023. The desks and file cabinets are still there, and that back storage room is still locked up with several years’ worth of his boxed client records from those 2021–2022 returns. I haven’t touched any of it while he’s going back and forth with the IRS audit office in Indianapolis and his attorney. Honestly it creeps me out a little walking past that door when I’m over there checking on 32nd Street Market and Westside Cuts & Styles.

On top of that, since Mom’s knee replacement at Memorial Hospital on January 9 this year, I’ve been using the back office in Suite B to stash some of her extra medical stuff so it’s not cluttering their garage on W. 32nd. Her old walker, the portable commode, three unopened cases of those incontinence pads, and that folding hospital bed frame from St. Joseph Home Medical are all stacked against the wall. I keep the blinds closed so the neighbors don’t see us loading things in and out.

Claire’s still helping out Maria at Westside Cuts & Styles on Wednesdays and Fridays after school, sweeping and cleaning shampoo bowls to chip away at that $620 orthodontic bill from Memorial Children’s Dental. I swing by around 5:30 to check her little timesheet and lock up the back door.

Let me know if you want to ride along next time I head down to 1542. I could use your eyes on that old panel again.

– Mike

metadata.subject: Quick update on Suite B and Mom’s stuff

artifacts.content:
Hi Ahmed,

Hope things are going smoothly at 32nd Street Market. I swung by 1542 W 32nd earlier this week and the place looked good – lot was pretty clean and the pole light in the parking lot was actually brighter than I remembered.

Couple quick things:

1) The little AA-style group using the rear storage area after hours on Tuesdays and Thursdays is fine to keep going as long as it’s still low-key. Dad (Tom) told me those 9:15–10:30 PM meetings have really helped him stay on track since his relapse back in late 2022, and his sponsor Bill seems to like the setup. I really appreciate you letting them come in through the alley door so it doesn’t interfere with your normal hours.

2) Since August 2023 I’ve been dropping that metal cash box in the corner of your back room – the one with the late rent payments and money orders from some of my South Bend and Mishawaka units. It’s usually around $3,000–$4,000 total. I’ll keep swinging by on the 1st and 15th around 8:30 PM to pick up or drop off envelopes so it’s not sitting around the house where the kids can see it.

If the meetings or the cash box ever start to feel like a hassle or crowd your storage, please tell me right away. You’ve been a great tenant and I don’t want to overstep.

Thanks again,

Michael Hanley
Hanley Property Group LLC
(317) 555-2843

metadata.subject: After-hours use of the back room

artifacts.content:
Personal note – Suite B / Sean / Mom

Need to get my arms around what’s happening in the middle suite at 1542 W 32nd so I’m not just letting it drift.

1) Sean / FastTrack
- Suite B has been sitting basically untouched since FastTrack Tax & Notary shut down in April 2023.
- That locked storage room still has several years of his boxed client files from the 2021–2022 tax seasons when the IRS audit blew up.
- I keep forgetting that the address on some of his paperwork is 1842 W. 32nd Street in South Bend, not Indy, which makes things even more confusing.
- To-do: Call Sean this weekend and ask what his attorney says about how long I need to leave those boxes in place.

2) Mom’s medical gear
- Since January 2024, I’ve been using the back office in Suite B as overflow storage for Mom’s post–knee replacement equipment from Memorial Hospital.
- Currently in there: old walker, portable commode, folding hospital bed frame from St. Joseph Home Medical, plus three unopened cases of her incontinence pads.
- Erin and I need to move at least the bed frame back to their house now that Mom is more mobile.

3) Future use
- Space is still basically the old office layout (waiting area + 2 offices + storage + restroom).
- Keep blinds closed for now so neighbors don’t see us hauling medical stuff in and out.
- Long-term: clean out, repaint, and get it truly lease-ready.

Erin,

I’m hiding in the spare bedroom between calls, so I figured I’d answer your question about Claire’s “job” before I forget.

Since around March this year, Maria at Westside Cuts & Styles (Suite C at 1542 W 32nd) has been letting Claire come by Wednesdays and Fridays after school. Claire sweeps up hair, wipes down the chairs, cleans the shampoo bowls, and helps restock product on the shelves. It’s nothing intense – maybe 1.5–2 hours each time – but Maria insists on paying her in cash. We agreed to keep the money in a little labeled envelope in the locked cabinet by Maria’s station so Claire doesn’t lose it.

The idea is for her to pay down that $620 orthodontic bill from Memorial Children’s Dental in South Bend. Claire actually likes feeling “grown up” and Maria says she’s good with the customers. I usually swing by around 5:30 PM on those days to check her scribbled timesheet, make sure the back door is locked, and walk her out to the car.

While I’m there I’ve been eyeballing the rest of the building – Suite A with 32nd Street Market is steady, but Suite B is still in limbo with Sean’s old FastTrack stuff and Mom’s medical gear piled in the back office. It’s weird how that one middle space manages to stress me out more than the rest of the property combined.

Anyway, Claire’s there again this Friday, so if you want to come by after work we can all grab tacos on the way home.

– M

Hi Laura,

Hope you’re doing well. I wanted to run a couple of zoning-ish questions by you about my little center at 1542 W 32nd Street in Center Township.

Most of the building is exactly what you’d expect – 32nd Street Market in Suite A (typical corner market, no fuel, no on-site alcohol consumption) and Westside Cuts & Styles in Suite C (hair salon/barbershop). The middle suite is vacant at the moment.

Where I’m getting nervous is the “gray area” stuff that has crept in over the last year. Right now a portion of the building is being used for small-time auto work and storing some construction equipment – basically my contractor buddy tinkering with brakes and keeping a couple of trailers and compressors inside when he’s between jobs. It’s not a full-blown garage, but I honestly don’t know if that sort of thing flies under the current neighborhood commercial zoning.

I also have some other non-tenant uses happening: my dad’s AA-style group meets after hours in the back of 32nd Street Market on Tuesday/Thursday nights, and I’ve been using the vacant Suite B to store my mom’s medical equipment since her knee replacement in January. None of this generates traffic or noise, but I’m trying to clean things up and get ahead of any potential complaints.

Could we schedule a quick call next week so I can walk you through what’s happening and get your read on whether I need to rein things in or file anything formal?

Thanks,

Michael Hanley
Hanley Property Group LLC
Indianapolis, IN 46260
(317) 555-2843

metadata.subject: Question about some “gray area” uses at 1542 W 32nd

artifacts.content:
Property to-dos – 1542 W 32nd + extra lot

1) Back of lot cleanup
- Behind the building, along the rear property line, we’ve basically let it turn into a dumping/holding area.
- There are two old storage containers back there plus leftover construction materials (broken pallets, a couple stacks of block, random scrap lumber) that never got hauled off after the last roof and fascia repair.
- Need to schedule a Saturday with one of the guys to rent a small dumpster, clear everything out, and maybe throw down some gravel so it doesn’t turn into a mud pit.

2) Overflow parking on the vacant side parcel
- The vacant lot next door has turned into de facto overflow parking. A couple neighboring businesses are leaving box trucks and utility trailers there overnight and on weekends.
- I said yes informally because it made the block look more active, but there’s zero paperwork and I don’t love the liability.
- To-do: talk to Laura about whether I should formalize anything or just tell them to stop.

3) Interior odds and ends
- Check Suite B again: Sean’s FastTrack boxes still in the locked room; Mom’s walker/commode/bed frame still in the back office.
- Confirm exterior lights on timers; Ahmed said the pole light flickered last week.

4) Family
- Ask Dad how the Tues/Thurs 9:15 PM group at 32nd Street Market is going. Make sure he still feels safe there.

Hi Carlos,

Catching up on a few maintenance things at 1542 W 32nd and wanted to see if you and your crew could help in the next couple of weeks.

Main priority is the mess along the rear property line. Over the last year we’ve kind of let that area behind the building turn into a catch-all. There are a couple of old metal storage pods sitting back there, plus leftover construction materials from the last round of fascia/soffit work – broken pallets, some half-used bundles, random lengths of 2x4. From the alley it just looks like a junkyard strip now.

I’d like to:
- Clear out the storage containers completely (we can decide whether to scrap or relocate them once we see what’s inside).
- Load all the loose materials and debris into a roll-off.
- Do a quick trim of the brush that’s starting to grow through the fence.

While you’re there, can you also take a quick look at the alley-side door to Suite B? I’ve been in and out more lately, moving some of my mom’s post-surgery medical equipment that I’ve been storing in the back office, and the latch feels loose. Nothing urgent, but I don’t want it to get to the point where it won’t close properly.

If you can give me a rough estimate and a couple of dates that work, I’ll plan to meet you on-site. Early mornings are usually best for me after I drop the kids at school.

Thanks,

Michael
Hanley Property Group LLC
mhanley@hanleypropertygroup.com
(317) 555-2843
